title: President

result_summary.name: TTT Power Transition 2026
result_summary.description: Canonical TTT template locked 2026-04-05 after Map+Units finalization. 20 countries, 10x20 global hex grid, 2 tactical theaters (eastern_ereb, mashriq), 345 default units.

result_summary.note: Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.

final_assessment:
## Military Assessment - Q1 2026

**Current Strategic Position:**
Sarmatia maintains an active war posture against Ruthenia with 32 active units deployed and 11 in strategic reserve. Our nuclear triad remains intact with 12 strategic missile systems, providing significant deterrent capability.

**Force Deployment:**
The bulk of our ground forces (12 of 16 active ground units) are concentrated in the Eastern Ereb theater, engaged in active operations along the Pokrovsk-Donetsk axis, Luhansk occupation zones, and Zaporizhzhia front. We maintain strong defensive positions in occupied Crimea and the lower Dnipro region. Our Northern Grouping covers the Kursk-Belgorod-Voronezh corridor.

Outside the theater, we have critical defensive positions: one unit securing the Kaliningrad exclave, one protecting the Arctic/Kola region, and our Moscow VO garrison providing homeland security.

**Air and Strategic Assets:**
Our tactical aviation (6 of 8 active) supports front-line operations with glide bomb capabilities and CAS missions. Air defense coverage protects Moscow, Crimea, and Kaliningrad with S-400 systems. Strategic missile forces are well-distributed with ICBM silos and mobile launchers positioned across our territory.

**Key Vulnerabilities:**
Ruthenia has concentrated their defensive "Die Hard core" around Kramatorsk-Sloviansk, creating a fortress belt that our ground forces are directly engaging. Their forces appear well-positioned for defensive operations with integrated air defense.

**Strategic Assessment:**
We hold territorial advantages in occupied zones but face determined resistance. Our nuclear deterrent provides strategic stability while conventional forces remain locked in attritional warfare in Eastern Ereb.

content_blocks.text: I'll assess Sarmatia's military posture by gathering the necessary intelligence. Let me start with our strategic context and force disposition.
